steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to contrast how the igneous rock at the two parks formed. A good answer must name both Acadia and Crater Lake, and explain the difference: Acadia's rock cooled deep within Earth's crust (intrusive); Crater Lake's cooled after exiting the crust (extrusive).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Acadia National Park contains examples of intrusive igneous rock; the other type of igneous rock is called extrusive igneous rock.’: Never mentions Crater Lake or how its rock formed. No contrast between the two parks. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Acadia National Park's igneous rock formed from molten rock that cooled deep within Earth's crust, whereas Crater Lake National Park's igneous rock formed from molten rock that cooled after it exited Earth's crust.’: Directly contrasts both parks' formation processes using “whereas.” Hits every required detail. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘Acadia National Park and Crater Lake National Park both contain igneous rock, which is formed when molten rock cools.’: Shows similarity, not contrast — the opposite of what the question asks. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Two types of igneous rock … are intrusive igneous rock and extrusive igneous rock, both of which can be found in national parks.’: Defines the two types generally but never names the specific parks or contrasts their formation. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
